name: payload_differs_from_input
code:
proof fn phi_1_payload_differs_from_input(
    pre: SingleDelivery<Message>,
    post: SingleDelivery<Message>,
    m: Message,
    dst: AbstractEndPoint,
    sm: SingleMessage<Message>,
)
    requires
        SingleDelivery::send_single_message(pre, post, m, dst, Some(sm), AbstractParameters::static_params()),
        sm is Message,
    ensures
        sm.arrow_Message_m() != m,
{
}
reason: If provable, the spec allows a successfully sent message to carry a different payload than the one requested

name: seqno_zero_on_success
code:
proof fn phi_2_seqno_zero_on_success(
    pre: SingleDelivery<Message>,
    post: SingleDelivery<Message>,
    m: Message,
    dst: AbstractEndPoint,
    sm: SingleMessage<Message>,
)
    requires
        SingleDelivery::send_single_message(pre, post, m, dst, Some(sm), AbstractParameters::static_params()),
        sm is Message,
    ensures
        sm.arrow_Message_seqno() == 0nat,
{
}
reason: If provable, the spec permits a zero sequence number on a successful send, but new_seqno must be >= 1

name: un_acked_no_growth
code:
proof fn phi_3_un_acked_no_growth(
    pre: SingleDelivery<Message>,
    post: SingleDelivery<Message>,
    m: Message,
    dst: AbstractEndPoint,
    sm: SingleMessage<Message>,
)
    requires
        SingleDelivery::send_single_message(pre, post, m, dst, Some(sm), AbstractParameters::static_params()),
    ensures
        ack_state_lookup(dst, post.send_state).un_acked.len()
            == ack_state_lookup(dst, pre.send_state).un_acked.len(),
{
}
reason: If provable, a successful send does not grow the un_acked list, contradicting the push in the spec

name: missing_key_nonempty_unacked
reason: If provable, looking up a missing endpoint yields a non-empty un_acked list instead of the expected empty default

name: some_value_always_valid
reason: If provable, every abstract value is considered valid, meaning valid_optional_value imposes no constraint on Some values
